items.q: What is the most common informal way to form a yes/no question in French?
items.correct: Rising intonation (statement said as a question) or using 'est-ce que'.
items.why: Students learn formal inversion first. In conversation, French uses intonation or est-ce que far more often. Inversion sounds formal/written - 'Tu parles français?' is the everyday form.

items.correct: As a nasal vowel - the n is not fully pronounced.
items.why: Students pronounce nasal vowels like English consonants. French 'on', 'an', 'in', 'un' are nasalised vowels - air resonates through the nose but the n consonant itself isn't articulated.

items.q: How do you say 'I was using my computer when the phone rang' in French?
items.correct: J'utilisais mon ordinateur quand le téléphone a sonné.
items.misconception: J'ai utilisé mon ordinateur quand le téléphone a sonné.
items.random_wrong: J'utilise mon ordinateur quand le téléphone sonne.
items.why: Students use passé composé for both verbs. The rule: ONGOING action (was using) = IMPARFAIT; INTERRUPTING action (rang) = PASSÉ COMPOSÉ. This tense pairing is critical for narrative writing.

items.q: Which sentence correctly says 'I get on well with my brother'?
items.correct: Je m'entends bien avec mon frère.
items.misconception: Je m'entend bien avec mon frère.
items.random_wrong: Je entends bien avec mon frère.
items.why: Correct answer: Je m'entends bien avec mon frère.. “Je m'entend bien avec mon frère.” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: Which form of 'you' should you use when addressing your French teacher?
items.why: The answer examiners want is: vous. “tu” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: How many forms does the French adjective 'beau' have (masc sing, masc plur, fem sing, fem plur, masc before vowel 'bel')?
items.why: The real figure is 5 forms. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
